name: panna cotta
instructions:
Bloom the gelatin in cold water until it softens and absorbs the liquid.

PLAIN / LEMON VERSIONS (cold method): combine heavy whipping cream and sugar with the bloomed gelatin, stir until the gelatin dissolves. If using lemon, stir in lemon zest at this stage. Pour into ramekins or glasses and chill until set (several hours).

CHOCOLATE VERSIONS (double-boiler method): for any version with chocolate chips or cocoa powder, warm the cream + sugar + chocolate in a double boiler until the chocolate melts and the mixture is smooth. Add bloomed gelatin off the heat and stir to dissolve. For the chocolate + peanut butter version, stir peanut butter into the warm cream-and-chocolate mixture before adding the gelatin. Pour into ramekins and chill until set.
notes:
Four lanes:
- PLAIN — no extras. Heavy cream + sugar + gelatin. The base recipe.
- CHOCOLATE — add chocolate chips or cocoa powder (or both). Use the double-boiler method.
- CHOCOLATE + PEANUT BUTTER — chocolate + peanut butter stirred into the warm mixture. The favorite version — peanut butter and chocolate are a foundational pairing and panna cotta is the right vehicle for them. Peanut butter only works alongside chocolate here; it isn't a standalone flavor lane (peanut butter on its own doesn't carry against the cream-and-gelatin base without chocolate doing the bridging work).
- LEMON — lemon zest only (no juice — would interfere with the gelatin set). Bright, clean, summery.

GELATIN: bloom it in cold water first. Adding dry gelatin directly to warm cream gives you clumps. Bloomed gelatin dissolves cleanly.

NO HEAT FOR PLAIN AND LEMON: these versions don't need a double boiler — the cream and sugar can be stirred together cold once the gelatin is bloomed and softened. The double boiler is only needed when you have chocolate to melt.

LEFTOVER FUSION: panna cotta uses pantry/fridge staples (cream, sugar, gelatin) plus whatever flavoring you have around (chocolate chips, cocoa powder, peanut butter, lemon zest). Easy to make from leftover odds and ends.
